paper_title: Sediment and Pore Water Analysis Methods

methods.description: Collection of sediment and pore water samples from the Cape margin using a Bowers and Connelly Megacore and Box core during the UK GEOTRACES A10 expedition.

steps.description: A Bowers and Connelly Megacore and Box core sampled shallow (o0.4 m) sediment and pore water from three sites on the Cape margin.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment and pore water samples using Megacore and Box core

steps.description: Subsamples for oxygen profiling and pore water extraction were collected by polycarbonate push core, and shipboard processing was performed at ambient bottom water temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: collect subsamples for oxygen profiling and pore water extraction

methods.description: Collection and preparation of pore water samples for elemental analysis using Rhizon samplers and subsequent acidification for metal analysis.

steps.description: Rhizon samplers collected dissolved pore water and overlying seawater constituents for elemental analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pore water using Rhizon samplers

steps.description: The aliquot for metals was acidified directly through the syringe tip and transferred to low-density polyethylene pots.
steps.searchable_summary: acidify pore water samples for metal analysis

methods.description: Extraction of highly reactive iron from sediment samples using a Na dithionite solution to target reducible iron oxide phases.

steps.description: A 10 ml aliquot of Na-dithionite solution was reacted with dry sediment sample.
steps.searchable_summary: react sediment with Na-dithionite solution to extract Fe

steps.description: The resultant solution was spun by centrifuge and supernatant containing dissolved Fe was decanted.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and decant supernatant for Fe analysis

methods.description: Measurement of element concentrations in pore water and sediment solutions using ICP-MS.

steps.description: A Perkin Elmer Element X2 ICP-MS measured the concentration of Fe and Mn in pore water, and Fe and Al in sediment solutions.
The accuracy of the method was verified by the intermittent analysis of blank-bracketed SLRS-5 within certified values.
steps.searchable_summary: verify ICP-MS accuracy with SLRS-5 standards

methods.method_name: Fe Isotope Determinations by Multi-Collector ICP-MS
methods.description: Assessment of isotopic composition of Fe in pore waters and sediments using a double-spike method and Multi-Collector ICP-MS.

steps.description: Sample solutions were quantitatively spiked with a 57Fe-58Fe double spike.
steps.searchable_summary: spike samples with 57Fe-58Fe for isotope analysis

steps.description: Fe isotope ratios were calculated using a double-spike data-reduction scheme.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Fe isotope ratios using double-spike method

methods.description: Determination of O2 concentration in surface sediments using Unisense equipment and linear calibrations.

steps.description: Linear calibrations were performed between aerated seawater and anoxic seawater before each use.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear calibrations for O2 profiling

steps.description: Data were converted to dissolved O2 concentration using empirical constraints.
steps.searchable_summary: convert data to dissolved O2 concentration

methods.method_name: Pore Water NO3- and NO2- Determination
methods.description: Determination of NO3- and NO2- concentrations in pore water using a colorimetric autoanalyser.

steps.description: Pore water samples were diluted with de-ionized water to a volume of 30 ml.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute pore water samples for NO3- and NO2- analysis

steps.description: A colorimetric autoanalyser was used to determine NO3- and NO2- concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze NO3- and NO2- concentrations using autoanalyser

methods.description: Assessment of bulk sample mineralogy using polarized light microscopy, XRD, and SEM with EDS.

steps.description: Polarized light microscopy, XRD and SEM with EDS assessed bulk sample mineralogy.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze mineralogy using microscopy, XRD, and SEM

methods.description: Calculation of total organic carbon concentrations from the difference between total carbon and total inorganic carbon content.

steps.description: Total organic carbon concentrations were calculated from the difference between coulometric determination of total carbon and total inorganic carbon content.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total organic carbon from TC and TIC
